# Prompt-to-Workflow Generation Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Let users type `r8r create "description"` and get a valid workflow YAML generated by an LLM, with preview, confirm, and refinement loop.

**Architecture:** New `src/generator/` module with three files (mod.rs, prompt.rs, llm.rs). The generator builds a context-rich system prompt from the node registry + credentials + examples, calls the LLM using the same provider infra as agent nodes, validates the output through the existing parser/validator pipeline, and presents it for confirmation. Two new CLI commands: `r8r create` and `r8r refine`. One new MCP tool: `r8r_generate`.

**Tech Stack:** Rust, reqwest (HTTP client), serde_yaml, existing r8r node registry, parser, validator

---

### Task 1: Extract shared LLM client from agent node

The agent node has provider-specific HTTP call logic (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama) baked into `src/nodes/agent.rs`. Extract the reusable parts into a shared module so the generator can call LLMs without duplicating code.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/llm.rs`
- Modify: `src/nodes/agent.rs`
- Modify: `src/lib.rs`

**Step 1: Create `src/llm.rs` with shared LLM types and caller**

```rust
//! Shared LLM client for calling AI providers.
//!
//! Used by both the agent node (workflow execution) and the generator
//! (prompt-to-workflow creation).

use reqwest::Client;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use serde_json::{json, Value};
use std::time::Duration;
use tracing::{debug, warn};

use crate::error::{Error, Result};

const DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 60;
const DEFAULT_HTTP_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 10;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, Default, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum LlmProvider {
    #[default]
    Openai,
    Anthropic,
    Ollama,
    Custom,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LlmConfig {
    pub provider: LlmProvider,
    pub model: Option<String>,
    pub api_key: Option<String>,
    pub endpoint: Option<String>,
    pub temperature: Option<f32>,
    pub max_tokens: Option<u32>,
    pub timeout_seconds: u64,
}

impl Default for LlmConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            provider: LlmProvider::Openai,
            model: None,
            api_key: None,
            endpoint: None,
            temperature: Some(0.3),
            max_tokens: Some(4096),
            timeout_seconds: DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECS,
        }
    }
}

pub struct LlmResponse {
    pub content: String,
    pub model: String,
    pub usage: Option<LlmUsage>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Default)]
pub struct LlmUsage {
    pub prompt_tokens: Option<u64>,
    pub completion_tokens: Option<u64>,
}

/// Call an LLM with a system prompt and user message.
/// Returns the text content of the response.
pub async fn call_llm(
    client: &Client,
    config: &LlmConfig,
    system: &str,
    user: &str,
) -> Result<LlmResponse> {
    match config.provider {
        LlmProvider::Openai | LlmProvider::Custom => {
            call_openai(client, config, system, user).await
        }
        LlmProvider::Anthropic => call_anthropic(client, config, system, user).await,
        LlmProvider::Ollama => call_ollama(client, config, system, user).await,
    }
}

/// Create a default HTTP client for LLM calls.
pub fn create_llm_client() -> Client {
    Client::builder()
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECS))
        .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(DEFAULT_HTTP_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SECS))
        .build()
        .unwrap_or_else(|e| {
            warn!("Failed to build LLM HTTP client: {}", e);
            Client::new()
        })
}

fn resolve_endpoint(config: &LlmConfig) -> String {
    if let Some(ep) = &config.endpoint {
        return ep.clone();
    }
    match config.provider {
        LlmProvider::Openai | LlmProvider::Custom => {
            "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions".to_string()
        }
        LlmProvider::Anthropic => "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages".to_string(),
        LlmProvider::Ollama => "http://localhost:11434/api/chat".to_string(),
    }
}

async fn call_openai(
    client: &Client,
    config: &LlmConfig,
    system: &str,
    user: &str,
) -> Result<LlmResponse> {
    let url = resolve_endpoint(config);
    let api_key = config
        .api_key
        .as_deref()
        .ok_or_else(|| Error::Node("No API key configured for OpenAI".to_string()))?;

    let model = config
        .model
        .clone()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "gpt-4o".to_string());

    let body = json!({
        "model": model,
        "messages": [
            {"role": "system", "content": system},
            {"role": "user", "content": user},
        ],
        "temperature": config.temperature.unwrap_or(0.3),
        "max_tokens": config.max_tokens.unwrap_or(4096),
    });

    debug!("OpenAI request to {} with model {}", url, model);

    let response = client
        .post(&url)
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(config.timeout_seconds))
        .header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", api_key))
        .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        .json(&body)
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("LLM request failed: {}", e)))?;

    let status = response.status();
    let response_body: Value = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("Failed to parse LLM response: {}", e)))?;

    if !status.is_success() {
        let error_msg = response_body["error"]["message"]
            .as_str()
            .unwrap_or("Unknown error");
        return Err(Error::Node(format!("LLM API error ({}): {}", status, error_msg)));
    }

    let content = response_body["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
        .as_str()
        .ok_or_else(|| Error::Node("No content in LLM response".to_string()))?
        .to_string();

    Ok(LlmResponse {
        content,
        model,
        usage: Some(LlmUsage {
            prompt_tokens: response_body["usage"]["prompt_tokens"].as_u64(),
            completion_tokens: response_body["usage"]["completion_tokens"].as_u64(),
        }),
    })
}

async fn call_anthropic(
    client: &Client,
    config: &LlmConfig,
    system: &str,
    user: &str,
) -> Result<LlmResponse> {
    let url = resolve_endpoint(config);
    let api_key = config
        .api_key
        .as_deref()
        .ok_or_else(|| Error::Node("No API key configured for Anthropic".to_string()))?;

    let model = config
        .model
        .clone()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "claude-sonnet-4-20250514".to_string());

    let body = json!({
        "model": model,
        "max_tokens": config.max_tokens.unwrap_or(4096),
        "system": system,
        "messages": [
            {"role": "user", "content": user},
        ],
        "temperature": config.temperature.unwrap_or(0.3),
    });

    debug!("Anthropic request to {} with model {}", url, model);

    let response = client
        .post(&url)
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(config.timeout_seconds))
        .header("x-api-key", api_key)
        .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
        .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        .json(&body)
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("LLM request failed: {}", e)))?;

    let status = response.status();
    let response_body: Value = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("Failed to parse LLM response: {}", e)))?;

    if !status.is_success() {
        let error_msg = response_body["error"]["message"]
            .as_str()
            .unwrap_or("Unknown error");
        return Err(Error::Node(format!("LLM API error ({}): {}", status, error_msg)));
    }

    let content = response_body["content"][0]["text"]
        .as_str()
        .ok_or_else(|| Error::Node("No content in LLM response".to_string()))?
        .to_string();

    Ok(LlmResponse {
        content,
        model,
        usage: Some(LlmUsage {
            prompt_tokens: response_body["usage"]["input_tokens"].as_u64(),
            completion_tokens: response_body["usage"]["output_tokens"].as_u64(),
        }),
    })
}

async fn call_ollama(
    client: &Client,
    config: &LlmConfig,
    system: &str,
    user: &str,
) -> Result<LlmResponse> {
    let url = resolve_endpoint(config);
    let model = config
        .model
        .clone()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "llama3".to_string());

    let body = json!({
        "model": model,
        "messages": [
            {"role": "system", "content": system},
            {"role": "user", "content": user},
        ],
        "stream": false,
    });

    debug!("Ollama request to {} with model {}", url, model);

    let response = client
        .post(&url)
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(config.timeout_seconds))
        .json(&body)
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("LLM request failed: {}", e)))?;

    let status = response.status();
    let response_body: Value = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| Error::Node(format!("Failed to parse LLM response: {}", e)))?;

    if !status.is_success() {
        return Err(Error::Node(format!(
            "Ollama API error ({}): {}",
            status,
            response_body
        )));
    }

    let content = response_body["message"]["content"]
        .as_str()
        .ok_or_else(|| Error::Node("No content in Ollama response".to_string()))?
        .to_string();

    Ok(LlmResponse {
        content,
        model,
        usage: None,
    })
}
```

### Task 2: Create generator context builder

Build the `GeneratorContext` struct that collects node catalog, credential names, and example workflows at runtime.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/generator/mod.rs`
- Create: `src/generator/context.rs`
- Modify: `src/lib.rs`
- Test: inline unit tests

**Step 1: Create `src/generator/mod.rs`**

```rust
//! Prompt-to-workflow generation.
//!
//! Generate r8r workflow YAML from natural language prompts using LLMs.

pub mod context;
pub mod prompt;

pub use context::GeneratorContext;
```

**Step 2: Create `src/generator/context.rs`**

```rust
use crate::credentials::CredentialStore;
use crate::nodes::NodeRegistry;

/// Context gathered for the LLM to generate a workflow.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct GeneratorContext {
    /// Node types with descriptions: vec of (type_name, description)
    pub node_catalog: Vec<(String, String)>,
    /// Names of configured credentials (never values)
    pub credential_names: Vec<String>,
    /// Example workflow YAML strings
    pub examples: Vec<String>,
}

impl GeneratorContext {
    /// Build context from the current r8r environment.
    pub async fn build() -> Self {
        let registry = NodeRegistry::new();
        let node_catalog: Vec<(String, String)> = {
            let mut descs: Vec<(String, String)> = registry
                .descriptions()
                .into_iter()
                .map(|(name, desc)| (name.to_string(), desc.to_string()))
                .collect();
            descs.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
            descs
        };

        let credential_names = match CredentialStore::load().await {
            Ok(store) => store
                .list()
                .iter()
                .map(|c| c.service.clone())
                .collect(),
            Err(_) => vec![],
        };

        let examples = Self::load_examples();

        Self {
            node_catalog,
            credential_names,
            examples,
        }
    }

    /// Load example workflows from the examples/ directory.
    fn load_examples() -> Vec<String> {
        let examples_dir = std::path::Path::new("examples");
        if !examples_dir.exists() {
            return vec![];
        }

        let mut examples = Vec::new();
        // Load a curated subset — keep it small to save tokens
        let preferred = [
            "hello-world.yaml",
            "order-notification.yaml",
            "smart-classifier.yaml",
        ];

        for filename in &preferred {
            let path = examples_dir.join(filename);
            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
                examples.push(content);
            }
        }

        examples
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_node_catalog_not_empty() {
        let registry = NodeRegistry::new();
        let descs = registry.descriptions();
        assert!(!descs.is_empty(), "Node catalog should have entries");
        // Verify well-known types exist
        let names: Vec<&str> = descs.iter().map(|(n, _)| *n).collect();
        assert!(names.contains(&"http"), "Missing http node");
        assert!(names.contains(&"agent"), "Missing agent node");
        assert!(names.contains(&"transform"), "Missing transform node");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_load_examples() {
        let examples = GeneratorContext::load_examples();
        // May be empty if not run from repo root, that's fine
        for ex in &examples {
            assert!(ex.contains("name:"), "Example should be valid YAML workflow");
        }
    }
}
```

### Task 3: Create prompt assembly module

Build system prompt and user prompt strings from the GeneratorContext.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/generator/prompt.rs`
- Modify: `src/generator/mod.rs`
- Test: inline unit tests

**Step 1: Create `src/generator/prompt.rs`**

```rust
use super::context::GeneratorContext;

/// Build the system prompt that teaches the LLM about r8r.
pub fn build_system_prompt(ctx: &GeneratorContext) -> String {
    let mut prompt = String::with_capacity(8000);

    // Part 1: Role and rules
    prompt.push_str(
        "You are a workflow generator for r8r, a workflow automation engine.\n\
         Your job is to generate valid r8r workflow YAML from user descriptions.\n\n\
         RULES:\n\
         - Output ONLY valid YAML. No markdown fences, no explanation, just YAML.\n\
         - Every workflow must have: name, description, triggers, nodes.\n\
         - Use {{ env.VAR_NAME }} for secrets and API keys. NEVER hardcode them.\n\
         - Use {{ nodes.<node-id>.output.<field> }} to reference previous node output.\n\
         - Use {{ input.<field> }} for workflow input parameters.\n\
         - Node IDs must be lowercase with hyphens (e.g., fetch-data, send-alert).\n\
         - Every node except the first must have depends_on.\n\
         - Add retry config for HTTP and external calls.\n\n",
    );

    // Part 2: Node catalog
    prompt.push_str("AVAILABLE NODE TYPES:\n\n");
    for (name, description) in &ctx.node_catalog {
        prompt.push_str(&format!("- {}: {}\n", name, description));
    }
    prompt.push('\n');

    // Part 3: Available credentials
    if !ctx.credential_names.is_empty() {
        prompt.push_str("CONFIGURED CREDENTIALS (use these names in credential fields):\n");
        for name in &ctx.credential_names {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("- {}\n", name));
        }
        prompt.push('\n');
    }

    // Part 4: Examples
    if !ctx.examples.is_empty() {
        prompt.push_str("EXAMPLE WORKFLOWS:\n\n");
        for (i, example) in ctx.examples.iter().enumerate() {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("--- Example {} ---\n{}\n\n", i + 1, example));
        }
    }

    prompt
}

/// Build the user prompt for creating a new workflow.
pub fn build_create_prompt(user_prompt: &str) -> String {
    format!(
        "Generate an r8r workflow YAML for the following:\n\n{}\n\n\
         Output ONLY the YAML, nothing else.",
        user_prompt
    )
}

/// Build the user prompt for refining an existing workflow.
pub fn build_refine_prompt(current_yaml: &str, user_prompt: &str) -> String {
    format!(
        "Here is an existing r8r workflow:\n\n{}\n\n\
         The user wants to change it as follows:\n\n{}\n\n\
         Output the complete updated YAML, nothing else.",
        current_yaml, user_prompt
    )
}

/// Build the user prompt for auto-fixing validation errors.
pub fn build_fix_prompt(yaml: &str, errors: &str) -> String {
    format!(
        "The following r8r workflow YAML has validation errors:\n\n{}\n\n\
         Errors:\n{}\n\n\
         Fix the errors and output the corrected YAML only.",
        yaml, errors
    )
}

/// Extract YAML from LLM response, stripping markdown fences if present.
pub fn extract_yaml(response: &str) -> &str {
    let trimmed = response.trim();
    // Strip ```yaml ... ``` fences
    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("```yaml") {
        if let Some(yaml) = rest.strip_suffix("```") {
            return yaml.trim();
        }
    }
    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("```") {
        if let Some(yaml) = rest.strip_suffix("```") {
            return yaml.trim();
        }
    }
    trimmed
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use crate::generator::context::GeneratorContext;

    fn test_context() -> GeneratorContext {
        GeneratorContext {
            node_catalog: vec![
                ("http".to_string(), "Make HTTP requests".to_string()),
                ("transform".to_string(), "Transform data with Rhai".to_string()),
                ("agent".to_string(), "Call LLM providers".to_string()),
            ],
            credential_names: vec!["openai".to_string(), "slack-webhook".to_string()],
            examples: vec!["name: hello\nnodes:\n  - id: greet\n    type: transform".to_string()],
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_system_prompt_includes_all_nodes() {
        let ctx = test_context();
        let prompt = build_system_prompt(&ctx);
        assert!(prompt.contains("http: Make HTTP requests"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("transform: Transform data with Rhai"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("agent: Call LLM providers"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_system_prompt_includes_credentials() {
        let ctx = test_context();
        let prompt = build_system_prompt(&ctx);
        assert!(prompt.contains("openai"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("slack-webhook"));
        assert!(!prompt.contains("CONFIGURED CREDENTIALS") == false || prompt.contains("CONFIGURED CREDENTIALS"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_system_prompt_includes_examples() {
        let ctx = test_context();
        let prompt = build_system_prompt(&ctx);
        assert!(prompt.contains("Example 1"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("name: hello"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_system_prompt_no_credentials() {
        let ctx = GeneratorContext {
            node_catalog: vec![("http".to_string(), "HTTP".to_string())],
            credential_names: vec![],
            examples: vec![],
        };
        let prompt = build_system_prompt(&ctx);
        assert!(!prompt.contains("CONFIGURED CREDENTIALS"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_create_prompt() {
        let prompt = build_create_prompt("fetch HN and send to Slack");
        assert!(prompt.contains("fetch HN and send to Slack"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_refine_prompt_includes_yaml_and_change() {
        let prompt = build_refine_prompt("name: test\nnodes: []", "add error handling");
        assert!(prompt.contains("name: test"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("add error handling"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_extract_yaml_strips_fences() {
        assert_eq!(extract_yaml("```yaml\nname: test\n```"), "name: test");
        assert_eq!(extract_yaml("```\nname: test\n```"), "name: test");
        assert_eq!(extract_yaml("name: test"), "name: test");
        assert_eq!(extract_yaml("  name: test  "), "name: test");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_fix_prompt() {
        let prompt = build_fix_prompt("name: bad", "Unknown node type: ftp");
        assert!(prompt.contains("name: bad"));
        assert!(prompt.contains("Unknown node type: ftp"));
    }
}
```

### Task 4: Create the generator orchestrator

Wire context + prompt + LLM + validation into the generate/refine flow.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/generator/mod.rs`
- Test: inline unit tests

**Step 1: Update `src/generator/mod.rs` with generate/refine functions**

```rust
//! Prompt-to-workflow generation.

pub mod context;
pub mod prompt;

pub use context::GeneratorContext;

use crate::error::{Error, Result};
use crate::llm::{self, LlmConfig, LlmProvider};
use crate::workflow::{parse_workflow, validate_workflow};
use tracing::{debug, info, warn};

/// Result of a generation attempt.
pub struct GenerateResult {
    /// The generated YAML string.
    pub yaml: String,
    /// Human-readable summary (e.g., "3 nodes: http → transform → slack").
    pub summary: String,
    /// Whether the YAML passed validation.
    pub valid: bool,
    /// Validation errors, if any.
    pub errors: Vec<String>,
}

/// Resolve LLM config from environment and credentials.
pub async fn resolve_llm_config() -> Result<LlmConfig> {
    let provider = match std::env::var("R8R_GENERATOR_PROVIDER")
        .or_else(|_| std::env::var("R8R_AGENT_PROVIDER"))
    {
        Ok(p) => match p.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "openai" => LlmProvider::Openai,
            "anthropic" => LlmProvider::Anthropic,
            "ollama" => LlmProvider::Ollama,
            "custom" => LlmProvider::Custom,
            _ => {
                return Err(Error::Node(format!(
                    "Unknown provider '{}'. Use: openai, anthropic, ollama, custom",
                    p
                )));
            }
        },
        Err(_) => LlmProvider::Openai,
    };

    let model = std::env::var("R8R_GENERATOR_MODEL")
        .or_else(|_| std::env::var("R8R_AGENT_MODEL"))
        .ok();

    let api_key = std::env::var("R8R_GENERATOR_API_KEY")
        .or_else(|_| std::env::var("R8R_AGENT_API_KEY"))
        .ok();

    let endpoint = std::env::var("R8R_GENERATOR_ENDPOINT")
        .or_else(|_| std::env::var("R8R_AGENT_ENDPOINT"))
        .ok();

    // For non-Ollama providers, API key is required
    if api_key.is_none() && provider != LlmProvider::Ollama {
        return Err(Error::Node(format!(
            "No API key configured. Set R8R_AGENT_API_KEY or R8R_GENERATOR_API_KEY environment variable."
        )));
    }

    Ok(LlmConfig {
        provider,
        model,
        api_key,
        endpoint,
        temperature: Some(0.3),
        max_tokens: Some(4096),
        timeout_seconds: 60,
    })
}

/// Generate a workflow from a natural language prompt.
pub async fn generate(user_prompt: &str) -> Result<GenerateResult> {
    let llm_config = resolve_llm_config().await?;
    let ctx = GeneratorContext::build().await;
    let client = llm::create_llm_client();

    let system = prompt::build_system_prompt(&ctx);
    let user_msg = prompt::build_create_prompt(user_prompt);

    info!("Generating workflow from prompt: {}", user_prompt);
    let response = llm::call_llm(&client, &llm_config, &system, &user_msg).await?;
    let yaml = prompt::extract_yaml(&response.content).to_string();

    // Validate and optionally auto-fix
    validate_or_fix(&client, &llm_config, &system, yaml).await
}

/// Refine an existing workflow with a change description.
pub async fn refine(current_yaml: &str, user_prompt: &str) -> Result<GenerateResult> {
    let llm_config = resolve_llm_config().await?;
    let ctx = GeneratorContext::build().await;
    let client = llm::create_llm_client();

    let system = prompt::build_system_prompt(&ctx);
    let user_msg = prompt::build_refine_prompt(current_yaml, user_prompt);

    info!("Refining workflow: {}", user_prompt);
    let response = llm::call_llm(&client, &llm_config, &system, &user_msg).await?;
    let yaml = prompt::extract_yaml(&response.content).to_string();

    validate_or_fix(&client, &llm_config, &system, yaml).await
}

/// Validate YAML. If invalid, attempt one auto-fix via LLM.
async fn validate_or_fix(
    client: &reqwest::Client,
    llm_config: &LlmConfig,
    system: &str,
    yaml: String,
) -> Result<GenerateResult> {
    match try_validate(&yaml) {
        Ok(summary) => Ok(GenerateResult {
            yaml,
            summary,
            valid: true,
            errors: vec![],
        }),
        Err(errors) => {
            warn!("Generated YAML has errors, attempting auto-fix: {:?}", errors);
            let error_str = errors.join("\n");
            let fix_msg = prompt::build_fix_prompt(&yaml, &error_str);

            match llm::call_llm(client, llm_config, system, &fix_msg).await {
                Ok(fix_response) => {
                    let fixed_yaml = prompt::extract_yaml(&fix_response.content).to_string();
                    match try_validate(&fixed_yaml) {
                        Ok(summary) => {
                            info!("Auto-fix succeeded");
                            Ok(GenerateResult {
                                yaml: fixed_yaml,
                                summary,
                                valid: true,
                                errors: vec![],
                            })
                        }
                        Err(fix_errors) => {
                            warn!("Auto-fix failed, returning with errors");
                            Ok(GenerateResult {
                                yaml: fixed_yaml,
                                summary: String::new(),
                                valid: false,
                                errors: fix_errors,
                            })
                        }
                    }
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    warn!("Auto-fix LLM call failed: {}", e);
                    Ok(GenerateResult {
                        yaml,
                        summary: String::new(),
                        valid: false,
                        errors,
                    })
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Try to parse and validate YAML. Returns summary on success, error list on failure.
fn try_validate(yaml: &str) -> std::result::Result<String, Vec<String>> {
    let workflow = match parse_workflow(yaml) {
        Ok(wf) => wf,
        Err(e) => return Err(vec![format!("YAML parse error: {}", e)]),
    };

    if let Err(e) = validate_workflow(&workflow) {
        return Err(vec![format!("Validation error: {}", e)]);
    }

    // Build summary
    let node_types: Vec<&str> = workflow.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.node_type.as_str()).collect();
    let summary = format!(
        "\"{}\" with {} nodes: {}",
        workflow.name,
        workflow.nodes.len(),
        node_types.join(" → ")
    );

    Ok(summary)
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_try_validate_valid_yaml() {
        let yaml = "name: test-workflow\nnodes:\n  - id: step1\n    type: transform\n    config:\n      expression: '\"hello\"'\n";
        let result = try_validate(yaml);
        assert!(result.is_ok(), "Expected valid: {:?}", result);
        let summary = result.unwrap();
        assert!(summary.contains("test-workflow"));
        assert!(summary.contains("1 nodes"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_try_validate_invalid_yaml() {
        let yaml = "this is not yaml: [[[";
        let result = try_validate(yaml);
        assert!(result.is_err());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_try_validate_valid_yaml_invalid_workflow() {
        let yaml = "name: \nnodes: []";
        let result = try_validate(yaml);
        assert!(result.is_err());
    }
}
```

**Step 2: Run tests**

Run: `cargo test generator`
Expected: PASS

git commit -m "feat: add generator orchestrator with generate, refine, and auto-fix"
```

### Task 5: Add `r8r create` CLI command

Wire the generator into the CLI with the preview+confirm+feedback loop.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/main.rs`

**Step 1: Add the `Create` variant to the `Commands` enum**

Find the `Commands` enum in `src/main.rs` and add:

```rust
/// Generate a workflow from a natural language description
Create {
    /// Natural language description of the workflow to create
    prompt: Vec<String>,
},
```

**Step 2: Add the command handler function**

```rust
async fn cmd_create(prompt_parts: &[String]) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    use r8r::generator;
    use r8r::storage::StoredWorkflow;
    use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};

    let user_prompt = prompt_parts.join(" ");
    if user_prompt.trim().is_empty() {
        anyhow::bail!("Please provide a description. Example: r8r create fetch HN top stories and send to Slack");
    }

    println!("Generating workflow...\n");

    let mut result = generator::generate(&user_prompt).await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{}", e))?;

    loop {
        // Show the YAML
        println!("{}", result.yaml);
        println!();

        if result.valid {
            println!("✓ {}", result.summary);
        } else {
            println!("⚠ Workflow has validation errors:");
            for err in &result.errors {
                println!("  - {}", err);
            }
        }

        println!();
        print!("Save? [yes/no/or type feedback to refine] > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;

        let mut input = String::new();
        io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut input)?;
        let input = input.trim();

        match input.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "yes" | "y" => {
                if !result.valid {
                    println!("Cannot save — workflow has validation errors. Provide feedback to fix or type 'no' to discard.");
                    continue;
                }

                // Save the workflow
                let workflow = r8r::workflow::parse_workflow(&result.yaml)?;
                let storage = get_storage()?;
                let now = chrono::Utc::now();

                let stored = StoredWorkflow {
                    id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
                    name: workflow.name.clone(),
                    definition: result.yaml.clone(),
                    enabled: true,
                    created_at: now,
                    updated_at: now,
                };

                storage.save_workflow(&stored).await?;
                println!("\n✓ Workflow '{}' saved", workflow.name);
                println!("Run with: r8r workflows run {}", workflow.name);
                return Ok(());
            }
            "no" | "n" => {
                println!("Discarded.");
                return Ok(());
            }
            feedback => {
                println!("\nRefining...\n");
                result = generator::refine(&result.yaml, feedback).await
                    .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{}", e))?;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**Step 3: Add the dispatch in the match block**

Find where `Commands` are matched and add:

```rust
Commands::Create { prompt } => cmd_create(&prompt).await?,
```

**Step 4: Verify it compiles**

Run: `cargo check`
Expected: compiles

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/main.rs
git commit -m "feat: add r8r create command for prompt-to-workflow generation"
```

### Task 6: Add `r8r refine` CLI command

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/main.rs`

**Step 1: Add the `Refine` variant to `Commands`**

```rust
/// Refine an existing workflow with a natural language change
Refine {
    /// Name of the workflow to refine
    name: String,
    /// Description of the change to make
    prompt: Vec<String>,
},
```

**Step 2: Add the handler function**

```rust
async fn cmd_refine(name: &str, prompt_parts: &[String]) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    use r8r::generator;
    use r8r::storage::StoredWorkflow;
    use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};

    let user_prompt = prompt_parts.join(" ");
    if user_prompt.trim().is_empty() {
        anyhow::bail!("Please describe the change. Example: r8r refine my-workflow add retry with exponential backoff");
    }

    // Load existing workflow
    let storage = get_storage()?;
    let workflows = storage.list_workflows().await?;
    let stored = workflows
        .iter()
        .find(|w| w.name == name)
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Workflow '{}' not found", name))?;

    let current_yaml = stored.definition.clone();

    println!("Refining workflow '{}'...\n", name);

    let mut result = generator::refine(&current_yaml, &user_prompt).await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{}", e))?;

    loop {
        // Show the updated YAML
        println!("{}", result.yaml);
        println!();

        if result.valid {
            println!("✓ {}", result.summary);
        } else {
            println!("⚠ Workflow has validation errors:");
            for err in &result.errors {
                println!("  - {}", err);
            }
        }

        println!();
        print!("Save? [yes/no/or type feedback to refine further] > ");
        io::stdout().flush()?;

        let mut input = String::new();
        io::stdin().lock().read_line(&mut input)?;
        let input = input.trim();

        match input.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "yes" | "y" => {
                if !result.valid {
                    println!("Cannot save — workflow has validation errors.");
                    continue;
                }

                let workflow = r8r::workflow::parse_workflow(&result.yaml)?;
                let now = chrono::Utc::now();

                let updated = StoredWorkflow {
                    id: stored.id.clone(),
                    name: workflow.name.clone(),
                    definition: result.yaml.clone(),
                    enabled: stored.enabled,
                    created_at: stored.created_at,
                    updated_at: now,
                };

                storage.save_workflow(&updated).await?;
                println!("\n✓ Workflow '{}' updated", workflow.name);
                return Ok(());
            }
            "no" | "n" => {
                println!("Discarded.");
                return Ok(());
            }
            feedback => {
                println!("\nRefining further...\n");
                result = generator::refine(&result.yaml, feedback).await
                    .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{}", e))?;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**Step 3: Add dispatch**

```rust
Commands::Refine { name, prompt } => cmd_refine(&name, &prompt).await?,
```

**Step 4: Verify it compiles**

Run: `cargo check`
Expected: compiles

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/main.rs
git commit -m "feat: add r8r refine command for iterating on existing workflows"
```

### Task 7: Add `r8r_generate` MCP tool

Expose generation to agents via MCP.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/mcp/tools.rs`

**Step 1: Add the parameter struct**

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct GenerateParams {
    /// Natural language description of the workflow to create or change
    pub prompt: String,
    /// If provided, loads and refines this existing workflow instead of creating from scratch
    #[serde(default)]
    pub workflow_name: Option<String>,
}
```

**Step 2: Add the tool implementation to the `R8rService` impl block**

```rust
#[tool(
    description = "Generate or refine a workflow from a natural language description. Returns the generated YAML and validation status. Use r8r_create_workflow to save it."
)]
pub async fn r8r_generate(
    &self,
    #[tool(aggr)] params: GenerateParams,
) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
    use crate::generator;

    let result = if let Some(name) = &params.workflow_name {
        // Refine existing workflow
        let workflows = self
            .storage
            .list_workflows()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| McpError::internal_error("storage_error", Some(json!({"error": e.to_string()}))))?;

        let stored = match workflows.iter().find(|w| w.name == *name) {
            Some(w) => w,
            None => {
                return Ok(CallToolResult::error(vec![Content::text(
                    json!({"error": format!("Workflow '{}' not found", name)}).to_string(),
                )]));
            }
        };

        generator::refine(&stored.definition, &params.prompt).await
    } else {
        generator::generate(&params.prompt).await
    };

    match result {
        Ok(gen_result) => {
            let response = json!({
                "yaml": gen_result.yaml,
                "summary": gen_result.summary,
                "validation": {
                    "valid": gen_result.valid,
                    "errors": gen_result.errors,
                }
            });
            Ok(CallToolResult::success(vec![Content::text(
                serde_json::to_string_pretty(&response).unwrap_or_default(),
            )]))
        }
        Err(e) => Ok(CallToolResult::error(vec![Content::text(
            json!({"error": e.to_string()}).to_string(),
        )])),
    }
}
```

**Step 3: Verify it compiles**

Run: `cargo check`
Expected: compiles

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add src/mcp/tools.rs
git commit -m "feat: add r8r_generate MCP tool for prompt-to-workflow generation"
```

### Task 8: Integration tests with mocked LLM

Write tests that mock the LLM response and verify the full pipeline.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/generator_test.rs`

**Step 1: Write integration tests**

```rust
//! Integration tests for the generator module.
//!
//! These tests use pre-canned YAML (as if returned by an LLM) and verify
//! the validation + summary pipeline works correctly.

use r8r::generator::context::GeneratorContext;
use r8r::generator::prompt;

#[test]
fn test_system_prompt_contains_all_registered_nodes() {
    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
    let ctx = rt.block_on(GeneratorContext::build());
    let system = prompt::build_system_prompt(&ctx);

    // Must mention key node types
    assert!(system.contains("http"), "System prompt missing http node");
    assert!(system.contains("transform"), "System prompt missing transform node");
    assert!(system.contains("agent"), "System prompt missing agent node");
    assert!(system.contains("filter"), "System prompt missing filter node");
    assert!(system.contains("switch"), "System prompt missing switch node");
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_yaml_from_various_formats() {
    // Clean YAML
    assert_eq!(
        prompt::extract_yaml("name: test\nnodes: []"),
        "name: test\nnodes: []"
    );

    // With yaml fences
    assert_eq!(
        prompt::extract_yaml("```yaml\nname: test\nnodes: []\n```"),
        "name: test\nnodes: []"
    );

    // With plain fences
    assert_eq!(
        prompt::extract_yaml("```\nname: test\nnodes: []\n```"),
        "name: test\nnodes: []"
    );

    // With surrounding whitespace
    assert_eq!(
        prompt::extract_yaml("  \n  name: test\n  "),
        "name: test"
    );
}

#[test]
fn test_create_prompt_includes_user_intent() {
    let p = prompt::build_create_prompt("monitor HN and send to Slack");
    assert!(p.contains("monitor HN and send to Slack"));
    assert!(p.contains("YAML"));
}

#[test]
fn test_refine_prompt_includes_current_and_change() {
    let p = prompt::build_refine_prompt(
        "name: my-wf\nnodes:\n  - id: a\n    type: http",
        "add retry logic",
    );
    assert!(p.contains("name: my-wf"));
    assert!(p.contains("add retry logic"));
}
```

Run: `cargo test --test generator_test`
Expected: PASS

```

### Generate Workflows from Natural Language

```bash
# Create a new workflow from a description
r8r create fetch HN top stories every morning and send digest to Slack

# Refine an existing workflow
r8r refine hn-digest add error handling that notifies via email on failure
```

The generator uses your configured LLM provider (set `R8R_AGENT_PROVIDER` and `R8R_AGENT_API_KEY`) to produce valid workflow YAML, validates it, and lets you review before saving.
